payload.query: What are the strengths and limitations of the single-country case study? Should scholars in comparative politics be doing more or fewer of these types of studies?

articles.title: Jesse Jackson, Charismatic Champion of Civil Rights, Dies at 84
articles.abstract: An impassioned orator, he was a moral and political force, forming a “rainbow coalition” of poor and working-class people and seeking the presidency. His mission, he said, was “to transform the mind of America.”
articles.snippet: An impassioned orator, he was a moral and political force, forming a “rainbow coalition” of poor and working-class people and seeking the presidency. His mission, he said, was “to transform the mind of America.”

articles.title: The Feminist Case for Spending Billions to Boost the Birthrate
articles.abstract: Fertility decline is a devilish problem. What if the only solution is to treat parenting as a public service worth paying for?
articles.snippet: Fertility decline is a devilish problem. What if the only solution is to treat parenting as a public service worth paying for?

articles.title: ‘This Is Worse’: Trump’s Judicial Defiance Veers Beyond the Autocrat Playbook
articles.abstract: The president’s escalating conflict with federal courts is even more aggressive than what happened in countries like Hungary and Turkey, experts say.
articles.snippet: The president’s escalating conflict with federal courts is even more aggressive than what happened in countries like Hungary and Turkey, experts say.

articles.title: Read the Decision
articles.abstract: A federal judge temporarily blocked the Trump administration from ending Temporary Protected Status for more than 350,000 Haitians in the United States.

articles.title: How the World Sees America, With Adam Tooze

articles.title: Harvard Derangement Syndrome
articles.abstract: ​In my 22 years as a Harvard professor, I have not been afraid to bite the hand that feeds me. So I’m hardly an apologist when I say the invective aimed at Harvard has become unhinged.
articles.snippet: ​In my 22 years as a Harvard professor, I have not been afraid to bite the hand that feeds me. So I’m hardly an apologist when I say the invective aimed at Harvard has become unhinged.

articles.title: Read the Court Ruling on Trump’s Use of the Alien Enemies Act
articles.abstract: The ruling was a setback for the Trump administration on the mass deportation of immigrants, one of its domestic policy goals.

articles.title: Science Practice | A Study on a Robotic Hand Design
articles.abstract: A new robotic picker-upper can roam around freely, like Thing from “The Addams Family.”
